opinion.h1: CAPELOUTO v. ORKIN EXTERMINATING CO., 385 U.S. 11 (1966)
opinion.h4: CAPELOUTO v. ORKIN EXTERMINATING CO. OF FLORIDA, INC. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF FLORIDA. No. 316. Decided October 10, 1966.
opinion.p: The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
385 U.S. 11 MASLOWSKY ET AL. v. CASSIDY, CHAIRMAN, ILLINOIS HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES COMMISSION, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ILLINOIS. No. 574. Decided October 10, 1966.
Albert E. Jenner, Jr., and Thomas P. Sullivan for appellants.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a properly presented federal question.
MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS, MR. JUSTICE HARLAN and MR. JUSTICE BRENNAN are of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
